code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a single, fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a readable fragment; {object} will be substituted.
    # Helper to format numeric objects without unnecessary decimal places
    def _fmt(val: str) -> str:
        """Format numeric values and strip surrounding quotes from strings."""
        # Strip surrounding single or double quotes
        if (val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"')) or (val.startswith("'") and val.endswith("'")):
            val = val[1:-1]
        try:
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                return str(int(num))
            # Keep a reasonable number of decimal places
            return f"{num:.2f}".rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
        except ValueError:
            return val

    clause_map = {
        # Core airport facts
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        # Runway specifics
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        # Geographic / political information
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "language": "speaks {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to the party {object}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarters at {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {object}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {object}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {object}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {object}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {object}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {object}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "lies in ceremonial county {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "falls under jurisdiction of {object}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {object}",
        "currency": "uses currency {object}",
        "class": "belongs to class {object}",
        "division": "belongs to division {object}",
        "order": "belongs to order {object}",
        # Fallback
        "default": "{predicate} {object}",
    }

    # Build lookup tables
    subject_to_triples: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    object_set = set()
    for t in triples:
        subject_to_triples.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)
        object_set.add(t.object)

    # Find a root subject (one that never appears as an object)
    root = None
    for subj in subject_to_triples:
        if subj not in object_set:
            root = subj
            break
    if root is None:
        # fallback: just use the first subject
        root = triples[0].subject

    # Recursive builder that creates a chain using relative clauses
    def build_chain(current_subject: str) -> str:
        """Recursively builds a clause for a subject, embedding relative clauses when
        the object is also a subject."""
        clauses = []
        for t in subject_to_triples.get(current_subject, []):
            tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate, clause_map["default"])
            obj = _fmt(t.object)
            clause = tmpl.format(predicate=t.predicate.replace('_', ' '), object=obj)

            # If the object is also a subject, embed a relative clause
            if t.object in subject_to_triples:
                rel = "which"
                if t.predicate in {"leader", "owner", "headquarter", "leaderParty", "capital"}:
                    rel = "whose"
                clause = f"{clause}, {rel} {build_chain(t.object)}"
            clauses.append(clause)

        # Join multiple clauses with commas and a final 'and'
        if not clauses:
            return ""
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            return f"{current_subject} {clauses[0]}"
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1])
            body += f", and {clauses[-1]}"
            return f"{current_subject} {body}"

    sentence = build_chain(root)
    # Ensure the sentence ends with a period and has proper spacing
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
